channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Implements Full Maritime Blockade on Iran Amid Potential Negotiations
item.description: The United States has fully halted maritime trade with Iran, despite optimistic signals from President Donald Trump that talks on ending the ongoing conflict could resume in Pakistan within days. Trum...

item.title: Get to Know Élodie Yung, Star of 'The Cleaning Lady'
item.description: Actress Élodie Yung has been captivating audiences with her role in the crime drama series 'The Cleaning Lady.' But who is she outside the screen? This article from People Metro.Style provides a glimp...

item.title: Family dispute delays funeral of man killed in Richards Bay mill shooting
item.description: A week after a fatal workplace shooting at Mondi Richards Bay Mill, the funeral of 66-year-old Shan Govender has been postponed due to a legal dispute between his long-term partner and his former wife...

item.title: Alberta proposes 120-day review period for major energy projects
item.description: The Alberta government has proposed Bill 30, aiming to accelerate approvals for major energy projects by establishing a 120-day review window. This initiative targets projects of strategic economic im...

item.title: Family of American Influencer Seeks Explanation After Her Death in Zanzibar
item.description: The family of Ashly Robinson, a 31-year-old social media influencer from the United States, is calling for clarity following her sudden death while on holiday in Zanzibar. Robinson had travelled to th...

item.title: Iran’s Buried Military Infrastructure and Its Role in Sustaining Capability During Conflict
item.description: The recent conflict in the Gulf, now under a fragile ceasefire, shows that modern warfare extends far beyond airstrikes or missile exchanges. Iran’s Qeshm Island, strategically located in the Strait o...

item.title: Ellah Lakes Reports Strong Revenue Growth as Palm Oil Production Starts
item.description: Ellah Lakes Plc don report im highest revenue ever after e start early commercial activity for oil palm and livestock business. The Nigerian agro-industrial company make N146.66 million for the 17 mon...

item.title: Virgin Voyages Crew Report Poor Working Conditions Aboard Ship
item.description: A Virgin Voyages cruise ship, the Scarlet Lady, has faced scrutiny following reports from crew members detailing concerning working conditions. Multiple individuals have come forward alleging inadequa...

item.title: Waymo Starts Testing Autonomous Robotaxi Fleet in London Ahead of 2026 Launch
item.description: Waymo, the autonomous driving technology company owned by Alphabet, has started testing its self-driving vehicles on public roads in London. This marks a major step toward launching what could become ...

item.title: Delimitation Bill Sparks Opposition Concerns over Southern State Representation
item.description: The Indian government's proposal to increase the number of Lok Sabha seats to 850 through a delimitation exercise has triggered strong opposition, particularly from southern states. Critics argue that...

item.title: CleanPeak Energy expands with acquisition of superannuation-owned renewables firm following KKR investment
item.description: Sydney-based CleanPeak Energy Holdings has embarked on a significant expansion drive following a $500 million strategic investment from global private equity firm KKR last year. The company has now ac...

item.title: Calgary Métis woman cultivates tobacco and shares it with community for ceremonies
item.description: Julietta Sorensen Kass, a Métis woman from northwest Calgary, unexpectedly grew an abundant crop of Nicotiana Rustica tobacco in her yard, initially intended for her personal prayers and ceremonies. S...

item.title: Letters Discuss Political Unity, Policy Challenges, and Societal Issues in Canada
item.description: The letters to the editor published in The Globe and Mail on March 15 cover a range of political and social topics in Canada, reflecting citizens' perspectives on current governance and policy matters...

item.title: Kogi Community Calls for Support for INEC Chairman Amupitan Amid Criticism
item.description: The people and stakeholders of Ayetoro Gbede Community in Ijumu Local Government Area, Kogi State, have urged Nigerians to stop calling for the resignation of INEC Chairman, Prof. Joash Amupitan, and ...

item.title: Geopolitical Tensions and Supply Disruptions: The Future of Oil Prices
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran has seriously disrupted global oil markets, primarily due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz – a critical waterway for around 20% o...

item.title: India plans to increase fertilizer imports due to Middle East supply disruptions
item.description: India inazidi kuangalia njia za kuongeza imports za fertilizer kutoka Russia, Belarus na Morocco kutokana na kushuka kwa supply kutokana na conflict ya Middle East. China pia imeweka export curbs amba...

item.title: Over 65,000 Teachers Promoted Under Marcos' Career Advancement Program
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. announced that more than 65,000 public school teachers and school heads have received promotions since August 2025, a result of the government’s Expanded Career Progress...

item.title: Numsa rejects Eskom wage offer, warns of potential labour unrest
item.description: The National Union of Metalworkers of South Africa (Numsa) has officially declared a deadlock in its wage negotiations with Eskom, rejecting the utility’s offer of a 7% annual increase over three year...

item.title: Maharashtra mandates basic Marathi proficiency for all taxi and rickshaw drivers
item.description: The Maharashtra government has officially mandated that all licensed taxi and rickshaw drivers in the state must have basic proficiency in Marathi starting from May 1. This initiative will be enforced...

item.title: Presidency Fires Back at Aregbesola Over Criticism of Tinubu Government
item.description: The Nigerian Presidency don reply former Minister of Interior, Rauf Aregbesola, after him call President Bola Tinubu’s Renewed Hope Agenda a scam and asked the president to resign for failing Nigerian...
